summary: Split the early memory-card support code out of lbcardgame into a new lbcardnew unit and matched a substantial set of lbcardgame/lbcardnew functions. The split moved the 0x80019880-0x8001C600 text range and associated data/bss/sdata/sdata2 ranges to lbcardnew, leaving lbcardgame at 0x8001C600-0x8001D2BC. The PR added lbcardnew.c/.h/.static.h, updated splits/configure/symbols, cleaned lbcardgame headers/static data, and adjusted include users across gm/db/lb/un/sysdolphin. The decomp.dev bot reported +1984 matched bytes and 26 new matches; the listed broken matches were primarily functions changing units from lbcardgame to lbcardnew rather than true code loss.
what_changed: Added new source unit src/melee/lb/lbcardnew.c containing the former early lbcardgame address range starting at 0x80019880, including matched functions lb_80019880, lb_80019894, lb_800198E0, lb_80019900, lb_80019A30, lb_80019A48, lb_80019AAC, lb_8001B6E0, lb_8001C404, lb_8001C5A4, and lb_8001C5BC plus placeholders for still-unmatched intervening functions.
Added lbcardnew.h and lbcardnew.static.h, moving declarations and static structs lb_804329F0_t and lb_80432A68_t out of lbcardgame headers.

Reduced lbcardgame.h and lbcardgame.static.h to the post-split lbcardgame range, with a focused lb_80433318_t layout and more precise prototypes for functions such as lb_8001C658, lb_8001C820, lb_8001CAF4, lb_8001CBBC, fn_8001CC30, fn_8001CEC0, and fn_8001CEE4.

Implemented many lbcardgame functions in the 0x8001C600-0x8001D2BC range, including CARDProbe state handling, save-data timestamp string construction, save/load wrappers using the internal filename string SuperSmashBros0110290334, state transitions, archive loading for LbMcGame./NtMemAc, and GObj/JObj/CObj setup in lb_8001CF18.

Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt to create a separate lbcardnew.c split with text 0x80019880-0x8001C600, data 0x803BAAE0-0x803BAB60, bss 0x804329F0-0x80433318, sdata 0x804D3800-0x804D3808, and sdata2 0x804D7C80-0x804D7CB0; lbcardgame now begins at 0x8001C600 with later data/bss ranges.
Added lbcardnew.c as an Object(NonMatching, "melee/lb/lbcardnew.c") in configure.py.
Updated symbols.txt around lb_803BAB9C by shrinking its size from 0x80 to 0x50 and adding lb_803BABEC as an SJIS data symbol.

Updated include dependencies so files that use old 0x80019880-range card functions include lbcardnew.h; some files include both lbcardgame.h and lbcardnew.h because they use symbols from both sides of the split.
Changed gmMainLib_8015CD98 from returning void* to int*, and changed gmm_x1868.x1A54 from s32 to int, matching the use in lb_8001CE00 where *gmMainLib_8015CD98() is incremented.
smart_moves: Separated the source unit at a real object-boundary-like address split rather than keeping unrelated memory-card setup/new-card routines inside lbcardgame, which made both headers and static storage ownership clearer.
Moved static data structures together with their owning code: lb_804329F0 and lb_80432A68 went to lbcardnew.static.h while lb_80433318 stayed with lbcardgame.
Kept old function address comments in headers, preserving address-searchability across the split.
Updated all build/split artifacts alongside the C split, avoiding a common decomp pitfall where a new file exists but the linker/config still treats the old object as one contiguous unit.
Used more accurate prototypes in headers instead of broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where evidence from implementations was available, improving downstream matching and type propagation.
Used #pragma dont_inline around lb_8001CAF4 and a separate static dont_inline_helper to preserve call structure for matching-sensitive code.
Modeled fixed-size struct layouts with STATIC_ASSERTs and explicit pads, keeping bss/data addresses stable during the split.
Preserved Japanese save-data text as SJIS-aware data, reflected by the new lb_803BABEC symbol entry.
decomp_lessons: When a large object contains two logical systems, splitting by contiguous text/data/bss ranges can convert apparent match regressions into relocated matches in the correct unit.
A source split must update splits.txt, configure.py, headers, static headers, and include users in one patch; otherwise matching and compilation evidence becomes ambiguous.
After splitting, revisit all public prototypes. Several lbcardgame declarations became concrete return/argument types once implementation context was available.

Static headers should track the owning translation unit's local bss/data. Moving lb_804329F0_t and lb_80432A68_t to lbcardnew.static.h prevented lbcardgame.static.h from describing storage it no longer owned.

decomp.dev match reports after object splits need interpretation: the bot listed 9 broken lbcardgame matches, but the same functions appeared as new matches in lbcardnew, indicating relocation rather than lost decompilation.
Precise pointer return types can matter for clean implementation expression: gmMainLib_8015CD98 returning int* better matched lb_8001CE00's increment operation.
Small symbol size corrections in symbols.txt can be necessary when a data range is split and a previously oversized local object actually contains separate data such as SJIS strings.
naming_conventions: New unit name follows the existing lb subsystem file style: lbcardnew.c, lbcardnew.h, and lbcardnew.static.h.
Address-based function names were retained, e.g. lb_80019880 for lbcardnew and lb_8001C600 for lbcardgame.
Local callback/helper functions keep fn_ address names when address-derived, e.g. fn_8001CC30, fn_8001CEC0, and fn_8001CEE4.
Static object symbols retain address-style names such as lb_803BAB60, lb_803BAB74, lb_804329F0, lb_80432A68, and lb_80433318.
Unknown struct fields continue the x-offset style, e.g. x0, x4, x8, x5C, x60, x64, with comments/STATIC_ASSERTs carrying layout evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Object split at exact boundaries: lbcardnew text 0x80019880-0x8001C600 and lbcardgame text 0x8001C600-0x8001D2BC, with corresponding data/bss/sdata/sdata2 ranges adjusted.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around lb_8001CAF4 to control inlining and preserve the expected function body/call shape.
Introduced static dont_inline_helper in lbcardgame.c to keep lb_8001CC84's control flow closer to the target assembly.
Used explicit casts for ABI-sensitive calls, such as (void**) &lb_803BAB74 and (void**) &lb_80433318.x5C in save/archive wrappers.
Used fixed constants and literals from target behavior, including SuperSmashBros0110290334, LbMcGame., MemCardIconData, NtMemAc, ScNtcCommon_scene_data, 0xD, 0xB, 0x29, and 0x2D.
Kept partly matching functions as implemented but not fully matched: decomp.dev reported lb_8001C8BC at 99.30%, lb_8001C658 at 99.12%, lb_8001CC84 at 71.05%, and lbcardnew lb_8001C404 at 90.24%.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code rising to 33.05%, a +0.05% / +1984 byte improvement.

decomp.dev bot reported 26 new 100% matches, including lbcardgame lb_8001CF18, lb_8001CAF4, lb_8001D21C, lb_8001D164, lb_8001CE00, lb_8001CBBC, lb_8001C820, lb_8001C600, lb_8001CDB4, lb_8001CE78, lb_8001C87C, lb_8001CC4C, fn_8001CEE4, lb_8001D1F4, fn_8001CEC0, fn_8001CC30 and lbcardnew lb_80019900, lb_80019AAC, lb_80019A48, lb_80019894, lb_800198E0, lb_80019A30, lb_8001B6E0, lb_8001C5A4, lb_80019880, lb_8001C5BC.
decomp.dev bot listed 9 broken lbcardgame matches, but those names correspond to functions moved into the new lbcardnew unit.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching lbcardnew functions between lb_80019BB8 and lb_8001C550.
Find later PRs that improve lbcardgame lb_8001C8BC, lb_8001C658, or lb_8001CC84 from partial to full match.
Search for refinements to lb_80432A68_t field names and layout after lbcardnew split.
Search for additional memory-card file name or icon-data cleanups involving SuperSmashBros0110290334 and MemCardIconData.
Check whether duplicate includes in gm_1A45.c and gm_1B03.c were cleaned up in a later style-only PR.
